title: Selectors, Cardinality, Domain Types, and Extraction Drift · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in typed cardinality-aware extraction pipeline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Define selection root, selector support, expected cardinality, fallback, exclusion, and source evidence for each field.
Return explicit zero, one, many, invalid, conflicting, and fallback outcomes rather than hiding them behind Find and First.
Parse external strings through constructors for URLs, language, dates, identifiers, enumerations, and bounded text.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Selector Drift
steps.instruction:
Order the Selector Drift Authorized site auditing and Go HTTP evidence cycle for this debug task before implementing it.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Selector Drift.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Selector Drift assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward. For Authorized site auditing and Go Order the regression proof · Debug Selector Drift, keep Selector Drift connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.text: A debug Go team handles case 2408 during a keyboard-first accessible report review. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove selector scope, cardinality, domain constructors, field normalization, and drift evidence; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then change one tokenizer, goquery, selector, domain constructor, record, link, frontier, or trap case and retain rejected evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Track cardinalities, fallback use, rejection rate, representative fixtures, schema revision, and changed markup alerts.

content.text: Order work so Selector Drift has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

Build deterministic Go evidence to prove selector scope, cardinality, domain constructors, field normalization, and drift evidence; release evidence binds source, modules, build settings, binary or image, configuration, policy, and restored state; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Track cardinalities, fallback use, rejection rate, representative fixtures, schema revision, and changed markup alerts.

options.text: A minimal trace that preserves the preconditions, contradicts the misconception, and satisfies Selector Drift.
Another successful run of the same happy-path example. That claim does not survive the selector drift · fault isolation evidence boundary.

For selector drift · fault isolation, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Selector Drift
steps.instruction:
Append a compile-ready pure-Go function headed "// Evidence: crawler-go-selector-drift-waszlm" that uses case 3475 to prove selector scope, cardinality, domain constructors, field normalization, and drift evidence. Return observable changed-case evidence. Browser code must not open sockets, resolve DNS, import x/net/html, goquery, Colly, or robotstxt, access host files, retain credentials, launch processes or unbounded goroutines, or claim native parser, race, load, accessibility, privacy, legal, container, restore, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Go 1.26.5, x/net 0.57.0, goquery 1.12.0, Colly 2.3.0, robotstxt 1.1.2, original fixtures, custom RoundTrippers, httptest servers, controlled DNS and browser-service environments, race and fuzz runs, subprocesses, inspected binaries and containers, accessibility review, capacity, backup, restore, rollback, legal review, and release gates.

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Selector Drift.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Selector Drift into observable performance evidence. For Authorized site auditing and Go Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Selector Drift, keep Selector Drift connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.
